deck: The final installment of Dimagi's values series. COO Lucina Tse, Chief of Staff Gillian Javetski, Avni Singhal, and Simon Kelly sit down with hosts Amie Vaccaro and Jonathan Jackson to unpack why the team chooses the harder road when it leads to greater impact.
seo_description: Dimagi leaders unpack the value 'Embrace complexity for impact': choosing the harder, windier road when it drives greater impact for partners.
og_description: Dimagi's team on embracing complexity for impact: choosing the harder road when it leads to greater impact.

pull_quote.text: We can't do them by ourselves. We are providing components of that, but we need to work with our partners, and not just be a vendor and a supplier, but really partner with others, so that together we can raise the bar.
pull_quote.cite: Simon Kelly, Director of Server Engineering

bullets: The harder road by design: why Dimagi chooses the windier path when it drives greater impact, while always keeping the destination in mind
Complexity for impact, not for its own sake: running just as fast toward national-scale programs even when many get paused or canceled for reasons outside Dimagi's control
Out-collaborate, not out-compete: why partnering across the sector beats trying to win alone in a field without enough profit to compete on
Start with why: how a shared understanding of goals makes feedback easier to give and keeps teams aligned
Learning from failure: no-blame retrospectives, owning your own mistakes, and the discomfort of speaking up to make teammates better
Articulating values as Dimagi grows: why naming how the team works together matters as much as what it builds
A growth mindset in practice: leaning into new skills and continual learning as core to high-impact growth

stats.label: Dimagi team members joining the hosts to discuss the value
how long 'out-collaborate rather than out-compete' has been a Dimagi tagline

guests.name: Lucina Tse
guests.role: Chief Operating Officer, reflecting on how the value shows up externally with partners, at the company level, and for individuals.

guests.name: Gillian Javetski
guests.role: Chief of Staff, on pairing bold ideas with humility, data, and a willingness to take feedback.

guests.name: Avni Singhal
guests.role: Senior Customer Success Manager, on out-collaboration with partners and the culture of owning up to your own mistakes.

guests.name: Simon Kelly
guests.role: Director of Server Engineering, on starting with why, learning from failure, and partnering across the wider sector.
